title: Delicate Crisp Argentinian Raspberry Empanadas
description: A sweet and tangy Argentinian dish featuring raspberries and a hint of mustard, perfect for a brunch mood. Served on a traditional talavera plate, these empanadas are sure to impress.

ingredients.name: butter
ingredients.notes: chilled and cut into small pieces

instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine the flour, butter, and salt, and use a pastry blender to work the butter into the flour until it resembles coarse crumbs.
In a small bowl, whisk together the eggs, mustard, and sugar, and add this mixture to the flour mixture, stirring until the dough comes together.
Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead for 5 minutes, until the dough is smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 12 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Roll out each ball of dough into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
Place a tablespoon of raspberries in the center of each circle of dough, leaving a 1/2 inch border around the edges.
Fold the dough over the filling, forming a half-circle shape, and press the edges together to seal the empanada.
Place the empanadas on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and brush the tops with a little bit of water.
Bake the empanadas for 20-25 minutes, until they are golden brown.
Remove the empanadas from the oven and immediately plunge them into an ice bath to stop the cooking process.
Serve the empanadas on a talavera plate, warm or at room temperature.
